problem_summary: 2023 MCM Problem A continuous. Plant community through drought cycles, verifying biodiversity aids survival, solved with a spatiotemporal reaction-diffusion PDE plus stochastic drought forcing.
models_used: Reaction-diffusion PDEs, biomass PDE per species (quadratic water growth, linear mortality, diffusion) plus groundwater PDE (rainfall, drainage, quadratic consumption, advection, diffusion)
Stochastic rainfall/drought sub-model, 52-week seasonal sine, tunable drought timing/duration/severity
Method of Lines in Mathematica NDSolve
Lotka-Volterra and Monod examined and rejected
key_techniques: Spatiotemporal PDEs unify immobility, competition, dispersal, habitat reduction
Each sub-question mapped to one knob, species count, growth, mortality, rainfall, habitat size
Quadratic self-facilitation growth drives clustering and water conservation
Periodic boundaries plus random-seed initial conditions
Match simulated stripes to real aerial photos
why_it_won: Rejected Lotka-Volterra for a spatial PDE answering every sub-question in one model, with mechanistic findings, a diminishing-returns optimum, severity worse than frequency, habitat loss causing extinction, and stripes matched to real photos.
section_highlights: Summary front-loads model and findings, Literature Review rejects Lotka-Volterra and Monod as a design spec, Model Development derives the PDEs with table and diagram, Results run k=1 to 4, Sensitivity sweeps resilience/pollution/habitat, Conclusion answers the five tasks.
pitfalls_or_limitations: Dimensional inconsistency, implicit unit diffusion, advection not a divergence operator
Unbounded quadratic growth, no carrying capacity
Water-only resource, cooperation is a water-conservation artifact
Compute cost capped species at four, sweeps at two
Periodic boundaries clash with habitat reduction
reusable_patterns: Literature review as a design spec
Map each prompt bullet to one tunable knob
Stochastic scenario generator for forcing input
Method of Lines with NDSolve for nonlinear PDEs
